perturbation.original_text: LICENSOR grants plan_b for the term of this Agreement the right to produce, market and distribute Content to End Users (in the territory specified in appendix 2) through its own and its partner's platform.
perturbation.changed_text: LICENSOR grants plan_b for the first six months of this Agreement the right to produce, market and distribute Content to End Users (in the territory specified in appendix 2) through its own and its partner's platform. After six months, continuation of these rights requires written renegotiation and agreement by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by limiting the initial grant of rights to six months, after which renegotiation is required. This contradicts the term outlined in section 7.1, which sets a 24-month agreement term. This creates uncertainty regarding plan_b's rights to market and distribute content after the initial six months, impacting the overall term and enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise stated in the Appendix the term of this letter Agreement shall continue for twenty-four (24) months with the effective date unless terminated sooner or extended pursuant to the terms hereof ("Initial Term"). The Initial Term shall automatically be extended for an additional period of half a year unless either party provides the other party with written notification of termination of the letter Agreement at least 60 days prior to end of such period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the initial grant of rights to plan_b to six months, requiring renegotiation for continuation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states a 24-month initial term for the agreement. This introduces a significant inconsistency regarding plan_b's rights and the overall duration of the agreement, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' due to the conflicting terms regarding the agreement length.

perturbation.original_text: plan_b shall pay LICENSOR a share of its revenues as set forth in APPENDIX 2 ("REVENUES").
perturbation.changed_text: plan_b shall pay LICENSOR a share of its revenues as set forth in APPENDIX 2 ("REVENUES"). However, plan_b reserves the right to unilaterally adjust the revenue share by up to 10% based on their assessment of market conditions, providing written notice to Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an ambiguity and contradiction concerning revenue sharing. Section 4.1 states that plan_b shall pay LICENSOR a share of its revenues as set forth in APPENDIX 2. The added text allows plan_b to unilaterally adjust this share based on 'their assessment of market conditions.' This directly contradicts the fixed revenue share outlined in Appendix 2 and creates uncertainty over the enforceable amount. This contradicts APPENDIX 2, where fixed percentages are defined and could become a point of dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPENDIX 2 1 CONTENT, SHARE & TERRITORY 1.1 Contract name (for internal plan_b-ware use): Data Call Technologies, Inc. CONTENT LICENSOR TERRITORY TERMINATION SHARE Top News Headlines Top Business Headlines Science/Health News Entertainment Headlines National Football League National Basketball Association National Hockey League Major League Baseball NCAA Football NCAA Men's Basketball Professional Golf Association NASCAR Latest Sports Lines 45% Worldwide 24 months Latest Sports Headlines Thought for Today Market Details World Financial Highlights Weather: Current Conditions 48-Hour Forecast 7-Day Forecast Weather Alerts Doppler Weather Radar
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that revenue sharing is 'as set forth in APPENDIX 2.' The changed text introduces plan_b's right to 'unilaterally adjust the revenue share.' APPENDIX 2 outlines fixed percentages. The added clause directly contradicts the fixed revenue share by allowing unilateral adjustments, thus introducing ambiguity where certainty existed previously. This is a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall keep in confidence all material and information, including without limitation Content, received from the other Party and marked as confidential or which should be understood to be confidential, and may not use such material or information for any other purposes than those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall keep in confidence all material and information, including without limitation Content, received from the other Party and marked as confidential or which should be understood to be confidential, and may not use such material or information for any other purposes than those set forth in this Agreement. Notwithstanding this clause, either party may disclose confidential information to potential investors or acquirers without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a specific exception to the confidentiality clause. While section 6.1 generally requires parties to keep confidential information, including Content, received from the other party, the added sentence allows disclosure to potential investors or acquirers without prior written consent. This contradicts the initial clause and creates ambiguity as to which scenario applies, undermining the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations concerning information shared with investors/acquirers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall keep in confidence all material and information, including without limitation Content, received from the other Party and marked as confidential or which should be understood to be confidential, and may not use such material or information for any other purposes than those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction to the original confidentiality clause. The original clause states that 'Each Party shall keep in confidence all material and information...received from the other Party,' while the added sentence allows disclosure to potential investors or acquirers without prior written consent, directly violating the initial confidentiality requirement. This contradiction creates ambiguity and uncertainty, making the confidentiality obligation unenforceable in the specific case of disclosures to investors or acquirers. This matches the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.
